categories.name: Milk & Dairy
categories.description: Milk, ghee, butter, paneer, khoya, chenna

categories.name: Oils & Fats
categories.description: Coconut oil, edible oils, refined oils

categories.name: Cereals & Pulses
categories.description: Rice, wheat, dal, atta, maida, ragi

categories.name: Spices
categories.description: Chilli, turmeric, pepper, cumin, saffron, hing

categories.name: Vegetables & Fruits
categories.description: Green vegetables, green peas, sweet potato

milk-dairy.name: Detection of Water in Milk
procedure: Put a drop of milk on a polished slanting surface.
Observe how the milk flows.
Pure milk either stays or flows slowly leaving a white trail behind.
Milk adulterated with water will flow immediately without leaving a mark.
milk-dairy.pureResult: Milk stays or flows slowly leaving a white trail.
milk-dairy.adulteratedResult: Milk flows immediately without leaving any mark.
milk-dairy.healthRisk: Diluted milk reduces nutritional value; pathogens in unclean water can cause gastrointestinal infections.

milk-dairy.name: Detection of Detergent in Milk
procedure: Take 5 to 10ml of sample with an equal amount of water.
Shake the contents thoroughly.
If milk is adulterated with detergent, it forms dense lather.
Pure milk will form very thin foam layer due to agitation.
milk-dairy.pureResult: Very thin foam layer forms on shaking.
milk-dairy.adulteratedResult: Dense, persistent lather forms on shaking.
milk-dairy.healthRisk: Detergent in milk can cause gastrointestinal disorders, stomach irritation, vomiting, and diarrhea. Long-term exposure may affect liver and kidneys.

milk-dairy.name: Detection of Starch in Milk / Khoya / Chenna / Paneer
procedure: Boil 2-3 ml of sample with 5ml of water.
Cool the sample.
Add 2-3 drops of tincture of iodine.
Formation of blue colour indicates presence of starch.
(For milk: addition of water and boiling is not required)
milk-dairy.pureResult: No colour change (remains yellow/brown of iodine).
milk-dairy.adulteratedResult: Blue colour formation indicates presence of starch.
milk-dairy.healthRisk: Starch adulteration reduces nutritional value. Can cause digestive issues, especially in infants and people with starch intolerance.

milk-dairy.name: Detection of Mashed Potatoes / Starches in Ghee / Butter
procedure: Take ½ teaspoon of ghee/butter in a transparent glass bowl.
Add 2-3 drops of tincture of iodine.
Formation of blue colour indicates the presence of mashed potatoes, sweet potatoes or other starches.
milk-dairy.pureResult: No blue colour; iodine remains yellow-brown.
milk-dairy.adulteratedResult: Blue colour forms indicating starch presence.
milk-dairy.healthRisk: Starch adulteration in ghee reduces its nutritional quality and can cause digestive problems.

oils-fats.name: Detection of Other Oils in Coconut Oil
procedure: Take coconut oil in a transparent glass.
Place this glass in refrigerator for 30 minutes. (Do not keep in the freezer)
After refrigeration, coconut oil solidifies.
If coconut oil is adulterated, other oils remain as a separate layer.
oils-fats.pureResult: Coconut oil solidifies completely — no separate layer visible.
oils-fats.adulteratedResult: A liquid layer of other oils remains separate on top or bottom.
oils-fats.healthRisk: Cheaper oils may contain trans fats or harmful substances leading to cardiovascular disease.

oils-fats.name: Detection of TOCP (Tri-Ortho-Cresyl-Phosphate) in Oils
procedure: Take 2ml of sample of oil.
Add a little amount of yellow butter (solid).
Immediate formation of red colour indicates the presence of TOCP.
oils-fats.adulteratedResult: Immediate red colour formation indicates TOCP.
oils-fats.healthRisk: TOCP is highly toxic. Causes paralysis, nerve damage, and serious neurological disorders. Can be fatal in high doses.

oils-fats.name: Proper Winterization of Refined Salad Oils
procedure: Take 100ml sample oil in a bottle, cork tightly and seal with paraffin.
Submerge bottle completely in bucket containing finely cracked ice.
Add water until it rises to top of the bottle.
Keep bucket solidly filled with ice for 5.5 hours.
After 5.5 hours, remove bottle and examine oil.
If properly winterized, sample will be brilliant, clear and limpid.
oils-fats.pureResult: Oil remains brilliant, clear and limpid after 5.5 hours in ice.
oils-fats.adulteratedResult: Oil becomes turbid or cloudy — improperly winterized.
oils-fats.healthRisk: Improperly winterized oil may contain saturated fats that can affect cardiovascular health.

sweets-sugar.name: Detection of Sugar Solution in Honey
procedure: Method 1: Take a transparent glass of water. Add a drop of honey. Pure honey will not disperse in water. If honey disperses, it indicates added sugar.
Method 2: Take a cotton wick dipped in pure honey and light with match stick. Pure honey will burn. Adulterated honey will not burn or produces cracking sound.
sweets-sugar.pureResult: Honey settles at bottom without dispersing. Burns cleanly when lit.
sweets-sugar.adulteratedResult: Honey disperses in water. Does not burn or produces cracking sound.
sweets-sugar.healthRisk: Sugar-adulterated honey spikes blood sugar levels rapidly, dangerous for diabetics. Reduces antioxidant and antibacterial properties.

sweets-sugar.name: Detection of Chalk Powder in Sugar / Jaggery
procedure: Take a transparent glass of water.
Dissolve 10g of sample in water.
If sugar/jaggery is mixed with chalk, the adulterant will settle down at the bottom.
sweets-sugar.pureResult: Clear solution with no sediment at bottom.
sweets-sugar.adulteratedResult: White chalky sediment settles at the bottom.
sweets-sugar.healthRisk: Chalk powder consumption can cause digestive issues, constipation, and long-term kidney damage.

sweets-sugar.name: Detection of Aluminium Leaves in Silver Leaves (Vark)
procedure: Take a portion of the leaf and crush it between two fingers.
Pure silver leaves will be easily crushed and crumble to powder form.
Aluminium leaves will only break into smaller shreds.
Make the suspected silver leaf into a ball and burn it with a flame.
Pure silver leaves burn away leaving glistening balls.
Aluminium leaves are reduced to grey ash.
sweets-sugar.pureResult: Crumbles to powder on crushing. Burns to glistening balls.
sweets-sugar.adulteratedResult: Only breaks into shreds. Burns to grey ash.
sweets-sugar.healthRisk: Aluminium consumption can cause neurological disorders, Alzheimer's disease risk, and kidney damage.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Extraneous Matter in Food Grains
cereals-pulses.adulterant: Dust, pebbles, stones, straw, weed seeds, insects, rodent hair
procedure: Take a small quantity of food grains sample in a glass plate.
Examine the impurities visually.
Pure food grains will not have dust, pebbles, stones, straw, weed seeds, insects, rodent hair or excreta.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: No visible impurities — clean, uniform grains.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Visible presence of dust, pebbles, stones, weed seeds, insects or rodent matter.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Can cause food poisoning, infections, choking hazards. Rodent excreta can spread Salmonella and Hantavirus.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Dhatura in Food Grains
procedure: Take a small quantity of food grains in a glass plate.
Examine the sample visually.
Dhatura seeds are flat with edges and blackish brown in colour — can be separated by close examination.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: No blackish-brown flat edged seeds visible.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Flat, edged, blackish-brown dhatura seeds visible among grains.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Dhatura is highly toxic. Causes hallucinations, convulsions, coma, and can be fatal even in small quantities.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Excess Bran in Wheat Flour
procedure: Take a transparent glass of water.
Sprinkle a spoon of wheat flour on the surface of water.
Pure wheat flour will not show excess bran on water surface.
Adulterated wheat flour shows excess bran floating on water surface.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: No excess bran floating on water surface.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Excess bran particles float on the water surface.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Excess bran reduces flour quality. May cause digestive issues and reduces absorbability of minerals.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Khesari Dal in Dal
procedure: Take a small quantity of dal (whole or split) in a glass plate.
Examine visually.
Khesari dal has edged-type appearance with a slant on one side and square appearance.
Pure dal will not have such irregularly shaped seeds.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: Uniform-shaped dal seeds, no edged/square irregular shapes.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Edged, square-shaped khesari dal seeds visible.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Lathyrism — a neurological disorder causing irreversible lower body paralysis. Especially dangerous for children and malnourished individuals.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Added Colour in Food Grains
procedure: Take a transparent glass of water.
Add 2 teaspoons of food grains and mix thoroughly.
Pure food grains will not leave any colour in water.
Adulterated food grains leave colour immediately in water.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: Water remains clear — no colour released.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Artificial dyes can cause allergies, hyperactivity in children, cancer risk with long-term use.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Turmeric in Sella Rice
procedure: Take a teaspoon of rice in a glass plate.
Sprinkle a small amount of soaked lime (chuna used in pan) on the rice grains.
Pure grains will not form red colour.
Adulterated grains will form red colour.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: No red colour formation when lime is applied.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Red colour forms indicating turmeric adulteration.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: While turmeric itself is harmless, artificially coloured rice may contain lead chromate (see Test 33) which is highly toxic.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Rhodamine B in Ragi
procedure: Take a cotton ball soaked in water or vegetable oil (conduct separately).
Rub the outer surface of the ragi.
If cotton absorbs colour, it indicates rhodamine B adulteration.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: Cotton does not absorb any colour.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Cotton absorbs pink/red colour from ragi surface.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Rhodamine B is a carcinogenic industrial dye. Causes liver, kidney damage, and is a known carcinogen.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Chakunda Beans in Pulses
procedure: Take a small quantity of pulses in a transparent glass plate.
Examine the impurities visually.
Chakunda beans can be separated out by close examination — they differ in shape and colour.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: Uniform pulse seeds, no foreign beans.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Chakunda beans visible — identifiable by distinct shape and colour.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Chakunda beans contain toxic compounds causing digestive problems, vomiting, and liver damage.

cereals-pulses.name: Detection of Sand/Soil/Insects in Atta/Maida/Suji
cereals-pulses.adulterant: Sand, soil, insects, webs, lumps, rodent hair
procedure: These impurities can be identified by visual examination of the flour sample.
cereals-pulses.pureResult: Clean, uniform flour with no visible impurities.
cereals-pulses.adulteratedResult: Sand, soil, insects, webs, lumps, or rodent hair visible.
cereals-pulses.healthRisk: Causes food poisoning, intestinal damage, allergic reactions. Rodent contamination can spread serious diseases.

spices.name: Detection of Foreign Resin in Asafoetida (Hing)
procedure: Method 1: Burn small quantity of asafoetida in a stainless steel spoon. Pure asafoetida burns like camphor with bright flame. Adulterated will not produce bright flame.
Method 2: Powder 1g of asafoetida in glass container, add 1 teaspoon water, shake. Milky white solution with no sediments = pure.
spices.pureResult: Burns like camphor with bright flame. Dissolves to give milky white solution with no sediment.
spices.adulteratedResult: Does not burn brightly. Sediment settles at bottom when dissolved in water.
spices.healthRisk: Foreign resins can cause allergic reactions, digestive disorders, and liver toxicity.

spices.name: Detection of Papaya Seeds in Black Pepper
procedure: Method 1: Add black pepper to glass of water. Pure black pepper settles at bottom. Papaya seeds float on surface.
Method 2: Spread spice on white paper and examine — black pepper is brown with wrinkled surface and pungent smell. Papaya seeds have smooth oval shape, greenish brown/blackish, repulsive flavour.
spices.pureResult: All pepper settles at bottom. Brown wrinkled peppercorns with pungent smell.
spices.adulteratedResult: Seeds float on water. Smooth oval greenish-brown seeds with repulsive flavour.
spices.healthRisk: Papaya seeds have abortifacient properties and can cause liver/kidney toxicity in large quantities.

spices.name: Detection of Light Black Berries in Black Pepper
procedure: Press the berries with the help of fingers.
Light berries will break easily while genuine black pepper berries will not break.
spices.pureResult: Berries do not break easily when pressed.
spices.adulteratedResult: Some berries break easily — these are light black berries (adulterant).
spices.healthRisk: Light berries have no nutritional value and may be toxic depending on the source.

spices.name: Detection of Soap Stone in Asafoetida (Hing)
procedure: Shake a little portion of the sample with water and allow to settle.
Pure asafoetida will not leave any soap stone or earthy matter at the bottom.
If adulterated, soap stone or earthy matter will settle down at the bottom.
spices.pureResult: No sediment or earthy matter at the bottom.
spices.adulteratedResult: Soap stone or earthy matter settles at the bottom.
spices.healthRisk: Soap stone (talc) causes respiratory problems, digestive disorders, and is a possible carcinogen.

spices.name: Detection of Artificial Colours in Chilli Powder
procedure: Sprinkle chilli powder on the surface of water in a glass tumbler.
Artificial colourants will immediately start descending in colour streaks.
spices.pureResult: Chilli powder floats without releasing colour streaks into water.
spices.adulteratedResult: Colour streaks descend immediately through the water.
spices.healthRisk: Synthetic dyes (like Rhodamine B, Sudan dyes) are carcinogenic. Cause liver damage, allergic reactions, and are banned in food.

spices.name: Detection of Light Black Berries in Black Pepper (Alcohol Method)
procedure: Float the sample of black pepper in alcohol (rectified spirit).
Mature black pepper berries sink while light black pepper floats.
spices.adulteratedResult: Some berries float in alcohol — these are light black berries.
spices.healthRisk: Light berries are adulterants with no nutritional value.

spices.name: Detection of Saw Dust in Chilli Powder
procedure: Add the sample to water.
Saw dust will float at the surface of water while chilli powder will settle at the bottom.
spices.pureResult: Chilli powder settles at bottom; no floating material.
spices.adulteratedResult: Saw dust particles float on water surface.
spices.healthRisk: Saw dust has no nutritional value, causes intestinal damage, and may contain chemicals from treated wood.

spices.name: Detection of Starch in Asafoetida (Hing)
procedure: Add tincture of iodine to the sample of asafoetida.
Appearance of blue colour indicates the presence of starch.
spices.adulteratedResult: Blue colour appears indicating starch adulteration.
spices.healthRisk: Starch adulteration reduces the medicinal and culinary value of hing.

spices.name: Detection of Chalk in Common Salt
procedure: Stir a spoonful of sample of salt in a glass of water.
Presence of chalk will make the solution white and other insoluble impurities will settle down.
spices.pureResult: Clear solution — salt dissolves completely with no white residue.
spices.adulteratedResult: Solution turns white and insoluble matter settles at bottom.
spices.healthRisk: Chalk consumption causes digestive disorders, constipation, kidney stones, and reduced mineral absorption.

spices.name: Detection of Exhausted Cloves in Cloves
procedure: Take some water in a glass and put cloves.
Genuine cloves will settle down at the bottom.
Exhausted cloves (volatile oil extracted) will float on surface.
spices.adulteratedResult: Exhausted cloves float on water surface.
spices.healthRisk: Exhausted cloves have no medicinal properties (eugenol removed). May be treated with chemicals during oil extraction.

spices.name: Detection of Cassia Bark in Cinnamon
procedure: Take a small quantity of cinnamon in a glass plate.
Cassia bark comprises several layers — rough outer and smooth inner layers can be differentiated.
Cinnamon barks are very thin and can be rolled around a pencil or pen with distinct smell.
spices.pureResult: Thin, smooth bark that rolls easily like paper around a pencil with a sweet distinct smell.
spices.adulteratedResult: Thick, multi-layered, rough bark that does not roll easily.
spices.healthRisk: Cassia contains high levels of coumarin which can cause liver damage and blood thinning issues.

spices.name: Detection of Charcoal-Coloured Grass Seeds in Cumin
spices.adulterant: Grass seeds coloured with charcoal dust
procedure: Rub a small amount of cumin seeds on palms.
If palms turn black, adulteration is indicated.
spices.pureResult: Palms remain clean — no black colour transfer.
spices.adulteratedResult: Palms turn black from charcoal dust on adulterated grass seeds.
spices.healthRisk: Charcoal dust inhalation and ingestion can cause respiratory issues and digestive irritation.

spices.name: Detection of Argemone Seeds in Mustard Seeds
procedure: Take small quantity of mustard seeds in a glass plate.
Examine visually for argemone seeds.
Mustard seeds have smooth surface — yellow inside when pressed.
Argemone seeds have grainy, rough surface — black colour — white inside when pressed.
spices.pureResult: All seeds smooth, yellow inside when pressed.
spices.adulteratedResult: Some seeds with rough/grainy black surface that show white inside when pressed.
spices.healthRisk: Argemone oil (sanguinarine) causes epidemic dropsy — severe fluid accumulation, heart failure, glaucoma, and can be fatal.

spices.name: Detection of Lead Chromate in Turmeric Whole
procedure: Add small quantity of turmeric whole in a transparent glass of water.
Pure turmeric will not leave any colour.
Adulterated turmeric appears bright in colour and leaves colour immediately in water.
spices.pureResult: Water remains pale yellow; turmeric retains its colour slowly.
spices.adulteratedResult: Bright yellow colour immediately leaches into water.
spices.healthRisk: Lead chromate is highly toxic — causes lead poisoning, kidney failure, nervous system damage, cancer, and reproductive harm.

spices.name: Detection of Artificial Colour in Turmeric Powder
procedure: Add a teaspoon of turmeric powder in a glass of water.
Natural turmeric powder leaves light yellow colour while settling down.
Adulterated turmeric powder will leave a strong yellow colour in water while settling down.
spices.pureResult: Light, pale yellow colour while settling — normal turmeric colour.
spices.adulteratedResult: Strong, intense yellow colour left in water.
spices.healthRisk: Metanil yellow is carcinogenic. Lead chromate causes heavy metal poisoning. Both banned in food.

spices.name: Detection of Sawdust / Powdered Bran in Spices
procedure: Sprinkle powdered spices on the water surface.
Pure spices will not leave any saw dust/powdered bran on the surface.
If spices are adulterated, saw dust/powdered bran will float on the surface.
spices.pureResult: Nothing floats on water surface after sprinkling.
spices.adulteratedResult: Saw dust or powdered bran floats on water surface.
spices.healthRisk: Sawdust contains no nutritional value and may contain toxic wood treatment chemicals.

vegetables-fruits.name: Differentiation of Common Salt and Iodised Salt
vegetables-fruits.adulterant: Absence of iodine (non-iodised salt sold as iodised)
procedure: Cut a piece of potato, add salt and wait for a minute.
Add two drops of lemon juice.
If it is iodised salt, blue colour will develop.
In the case of common salt, there will be no blue colour.
vegetables-fruits.pureResult: Blue colour develops — confirms iodised salt.
vegetables-fruits.adulteratedResult: No blue colour — indicates common salt being sold as iodised.
vegetables-fruits.healthRisk: Iodine deficiency causes goiter, hypothyroidism, intellectual disability in children, and pregnancy complications.

vegetables-fruits.name: Detection of Coloured Maize Cob Tendrils in Saffron
vegetables-fruits.adulterant: Dried maize cob tendrils soaked in sugar and coal tar colour
procedure: Take a transparent glass of water and add small quantity of saffron.
Pure saffron continues to give its saffron colour as long as it lasts.
If adulterated, artificial colour dissolves in water rapidly.
Note: Genuine saffron will not break easily like artificial.
vegetables-fruits.pureResult: Saffron slowly and continuously releases natural colour.
vegetables-fruits.adulteratedResult: Colour dissolves rapidly and completely in water.
vegetables-fruits.healthRisk: Coal tar dyes are carcinogenic. Fake saffron provides no health benefits and may cause toxicity.

vegetables-fruits.name: Detection of Malachite Green in Green Vegetables
procedure: Method 1: Take a cotton piece soaked in water or vegetable oil (separately). Rub the outer green surface of vegetable/chilli. If cotton turns green, malachite green is present.
Method 2: Place small part of sample on moistened white blotting paper. Impression of colour indicates use of malachite green or artificial colour.
vegetables-fruits.pureResult: Cotton does not turn green. No colour impression on blotting paper.
vegetables-fruits.adulteratedResult: Cotton turns green OR blotting paper shows colour impression.
vegetables-fruits.healthRisk: Malachite green is highly toxic and carcinogenic. Causes liver, kidney damage, reproductive toxicity, and is banned in food.

vegetables-fruits.name: Detection of Artificial Colour on Green Peas
procedure: Take little amount of green peas in a transparent glass.
Add water and mix well.
Let it stand for half an hour.
Clear separation of colour in water indicates adulteration.
vegetables-fruits.pureResult: Water remains clear after half an hour.
vegetables-fruits.adulteratedResult: Colour separates and water turns green.
vegetables-fruits.healthRisk: Artificial dyes cause allergic reactions, carcinogenic risk, hyperactivity in children.

vegetables-fruits.name: Detection of Rhodamine B in Sweet Potato
procedure: Take a cotton ball soaked in water or vegetable oil (separately).
Rub the outer red surface of the sweet potato.
If cotton absorbs colour, it indicates usage of rhodamine B.
vegetables-fruits.pureResult: Cotton does not absorb any colour.
vegetables-fruits.adulteratedResult: Cotton absorbs pink/red colour indicating rhodamine B.
vegetables-fruits.healthRisk: Rhodamine B is a carcinogenic industrial dye. Causes serious liver and kidney damage.

beverages.name: Detection of Clay in Coffee Powder
procedure: Add ½ teaspoon of coffee powder in a transparent glass of water.
Stir for a minute and keep aside for 5 minutes.
Observe the glass at the bottom.
Pure coffee powder will not leave any clay particles at bottom.
If adulterated, clay particles settle at the bottom.
beverages.pureResult: No clay particles at the bottom after 5 minutes.
beverages.adulteratedResult: Clay/mud particles settle at the bottom.
beverages.healthRisk: Clay consumption can cause intestinal blockage, reduce nutrient absorption, and carry harmful bacteria.

beverages.name: Detection of Chicory Powder in Coffee Powder
procedure: Take a transparent glass of water.
Add a teaspoon of coffee powder.
Coffee powder floats over the water but chicory begins to sink.
beverages.adulteratedResult: Some powder sinks — the sinking portion is chicory.
beverages.healthRisk: Chicory is generally safe but its presence reduces coffee quality. May cause allergic reactions in people sensitive to ragweed.

beverages.name: Detection of Exhausted Tea in Tea Leaves
beverages.adulterant: Exhausted/used tea leaves, coal tar colour
procedure: Method 1: Spread tea on filter paper, sprinkle water, wash under tap, observe stains against light. Adulterated tea stains the paper immediately.
Method 2: Place tea at centre of filter paper, add water drops. Coloured tea leaves colour streak on paper.
Method 3: Spread slaked lime on white tile, sprinkle tea dust. Red/orange/shades indicate coal tar colour. Genuine tea shows slight greenish yellow slowly.
beverages.pureResult: No stains on filter paper. No colour streaks. Only slight greenish yellow on lime.
beverages.adulteratedResult: Immediate staining of filter paper. Colour streaks on filter paper. Red/orange colour on lime.
beverages.healthRisk: Coal tar dyes in tea are carcinogenic. Exhausted tea has no antioxidant benefits.

beverages.name: Detection of Iron Filings in Tea Leaves
procedure: Take a small quantity of tea leaves in a glass plate.
Move a magnet through the tea leaves.
Pure tea leaves will not show any iron filings on the magnet.
If adulterated, iron filings will be seen on the magnet.
beverages.pureResult: Magnet picks up nothing from tea leaves.
beverages.adulteratedResult: Iron filings stick to the magnet.
beverages.healthRisk: Excess iron can cause iron toxicity, liver damage, and hemochromatosis. Metallic particles can damage the digestive tract.
